mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives corporate revenues and earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month increase >0.5% is followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals labor market overheating, which raises expectations of Fed tightening and wage inflation, depressing equity valuations over the near term.
failure_condition: If UNRATE < 4.5 is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, incentivizing borrowing and investment, which boosts equity prices over the near term.
failure_condition: If negative real rate condition holds but forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of occurrences over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust employment growth (PAYEMS rising >200k/month), rising retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher over short horizons.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month increase >1% during triggered periods is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 40% of cases over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above trend signals economic expansion, which lifts corporate profits and equity valuations over the medium term, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 30% of quarters over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 3.0 is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad demand contraction, weakening corporate earnings and leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline MoM for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If simultaneous multi-month declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE < 5.0%), rapid industrial production growth signals potential overheating, raising fears of monetary tightening and causing negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month increase >1% under UNRATE < 5.0 is followed by positive 20-day returns more often than negative over any 3-year window.
